paragraphs.body: You never expect grief until you live with it. The film Babadook explores this topic throughout the movie. I had not seen it until the end of my senior year of high school, during my English class. I learned from viewing that the entire film is about grief and dealing with it. The film is a horror film, but the ending is pleasant, even though the Babadook is only trapped in the basement. The story of the Babadook is a metaphor for grief. I did not realize it at the time, but the meaning of grief from the movie became very real to me only two months later.

The Babadook is a horror movie from 2014. The film follows a mother and her young son. On the day the boy was born, his father died in a car accident on his way to the hospital. Ever since then, the boy's birthday had been a painful reminder of the mother’s unbearable loss. When the son's next birthday was just around the corner, the weight of her grief resurfaced. One night, a mother reads a strange, spooky book about a creature called the Babadook to her son. The monster in the story is so terrifying that it immediately starts haunting the boy. Soon after, the creature starts to manifest itself in their lives, tormenting the small family. With the Babadook’s growing presence, the mother and son must work together to stop the monster. Instead of defeating or destroying the monster, they lock it away.

annotations.comment: I bet you could start your essay right here :). Also, great job with context. This is meaningful and important info for the essay!
annotations.context_left: The Babadook is a horror movie from 2014. The film follows a mother and her young

paragraphs.body: The Babadook is a symbol of the mother's grief as they do not kill or remove the Babadook, but it stays in their basement. This reflects the journey of the mother to accept her loss and learn to live with her grief rather than allow it to consume her and her son. The film ends on a hopeful note, where the mother and son celebrate the boy's birthday. It is a symbolic step ahead as they embrace life while coexisting with the past.

annotations.text: It is a symbolic step ahead as they embrace life while coexisting with the past.

paragraphs.body: Grief is an unavoidable part of life, something we all go through at some point. Grief occurs when you may or may not expect it. Whether spilling a hot drink on yourself or being late to a class, having a grievance is a genuine experience. Grief is not happiness. Grief is a deep, emotional, almost violent shift caused by harm or loss. It messes with the foundation of your life and makes you go through difficult emotions. These moments remind us that life is unpredictable, and it is always changing, no matter how small or significant the moments are. I learned about this reality when I was faced with many grievances.

For starters, high school was ending. That might seem like an exciting time for some, but for me, it was full of fear. I was afraid of losing my friends because of the distance and the life I was living. As humans, we fear change, and I was definitely scared. I was on the cusp of turning 18 and becoming an adult. I was going to be living alone, and I was going to be starting college. While my stress levels were high, my stress started to impact the people around me. After graduation, summer began, and packing and planning for one of the greatest changes in my life started. Then, one week into July, my uncle passed away from cancer.

annotations.text: Then, one week into July, my uncle passed away from cancer.

paragraphs.body: The grief and stress because of the loss was overwhelming. While few of us accept these changes with time, grief can come from an unexpected part of life that often comes along with grief. My uncle had cancer for many years, and we knew at the end of my senior year that his battle was ending when he entered hospice. Besides my own stresses from college, this development had an enormous impact on my whole family. Our house became quiet this summer.

annotations.text: Our house became quiet this summer.

paragraphs.body: My uncle battled cancer for a long time. I only vaguely remember him with all his hair.

Throughout his battle with cancer, he remained a steady pillar of strength for our family. He was someone I deeply respected and admired. Uncle Timmy’s cancer felt like a silent monster in our home. It lingered quietly, almost easy to overlook, but as time went on, its presence became impossible to ignore. The pain it brought was overwhelming, especially for my mother his sister, and witnessing her struggle was incredibly difficult. During that time, I understood how important it is to show up for family, and I did my best to be there for them.

annotations.text: Uncle Timmy’s cancer felt like a silent monster in our home. It lingered quietly, almost easy to overlook, but as time went on, its presence became impossible to ignore.

paragraphs.body: Now, it has been 5 months since the events of the summer, and I have started to understand the movie’s meaning. The Babadook never leaves, but the family learns to live with it. Grief never leaves, but now, 5 months later, my family is learning to live with our grief. I cannot speak for my whole family, but the phrase time heals all wounds has a lot of meaning for me. I look back on my time with my uncle, and I smile with happy memories of my family.

My uncle has always been a quiet man to me. He always had a chill vibe and was never overbearing. Every year, our small family got together in my grandmother’s even smaller yellow house for any occasion. Whether it was birthdays or holidays, we would always gather there. The house always smelled like my grandmother's. There is no way in words I know to describe the smell of my grandmother's old house except it smelled like her. When I enter my grandmother’s new house, I am reminded of her old house and the memories collected there, specifically of my uncle.

Collected on the small couch in the living room, my sister, my cousin, and I sat giggling, the whole house could hear our laughter. While we laugh and share our secret inside jokes, the adults are in the other room sipping their red wine with the background music of funky jazz combined with our obnoxious voices. When the wine was gone and the conversation got boring, my mother and her brother came to check on us. They joined us on that couch and joined our fun with a song. They would put their arms around us and rock us back and forth while singing “We Are Buddies” on repeat. Then, without warning, they would start violently shaking us while repeating it even faster. Before, we filled the house with our laughter, but when Mom and Uncle Timmy entered the room, our voices shook the house.

annotations.text: Before, we filled the house with our laughter, but when Mom and Uncle Timmy entered the room, our voices shook the house.

annotations.context_right: their arms around us and rock us

paragraphs.body: From my personal experience, grief is caused by loss, but grief can also be a reminder of our joyful memories with a person. Reflecting on the happy memories we had provides a bittersweet comfort amidst the pain of grief. It reminds us of the love that once filled our lives. In this way, grief creates a connection between our past and our present, allowing us to honor the importance of those relationships in our lives. The movie The Babadook reminds us not to get swept up in grief and to hold our loved ones close along with the memories of those who are no longer with us.

annotations.text: grief can also be a reminder of our joyful memories with a person. Reflecting on the happy memories we had provides a bittersweet comfort amidst the pain of grief. It reminds us of the love that once filled our lives.
